source.name: Hand-curated from CMVP Validated Modules List

notice: CMVP certificate numbers and status change over time. The 'verified_on' field records when the maintainer last confirmed the cert was Active. Re-verify before citing in any compliance deliverable. Active status can shift to Historical or Revoked at any time.

known_gotchas: GCC 14+ fails to build aws-lc-fips-sys; pin to GCC 13 or use Clang
macOS and Windows not FIPS targets — use Docker or Linux VM
Duplicate-crate trap: verify 'cargo tree -i aws-lc-sys' shows only FIPS variant
Do not also pull in the 'ring' crate — it is not FIPS-validated
Must install the FIPS crypto provider as process default before building a ClientConfig/ServerConfig
Assert config.fips() on every config you build

libraries.cmvp_certificate: pending (CMVP submission in progress as of 2026-04)
known_gotchas: Go 1.24 is the earliest version with native FIPS mode
Without GODEBUG=fips140=on, the binary runs in non-FIPS mode silently
Transitive dependencies may pull in golang.org/x/crypto with non-FIPS primitives — audit with 'go list -deps -m all'
BoringCrypto is a fork of Go, not a Go module — must use BoringCrypto toolchain
Falling behind mainline Go by a minor version or two is normal
For new projects on Go 1.24+, prefer native Go FIPS over BoringCrypto

platforms: linux-amd64 (via RHEL 9 FIPS / Ubuntu Pro FIPS)
known_gotchas: Python does not ship its own FIPS module — defers to system OpenSSL
Most macOS Python installs are not FIPS-capable; use Linux for FIPS
pycryptodome / pycrypto implement their own crypto — not FIPS-validated. Do not use in FIPS mode.
passlib with bcrypt/argon2/scrypt — all disallowed under FIPS. Use hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac with SHA-256.
Pre-compiled cryptography wheels on PyPI may bundle non-FIPS OpenSSL — prefer apt/dnf-installed system OpenSSL
libraries.activation: Requires OS-level FIPS mode: fips-mode-setup --enable (RHEL) or Ubuntu Pro FIPS subscription

libraries.certificate_name: cryptography library backed by OpenSSL 3 FIPS
known_gotchas: Verify the backend OpenSSL is the system FIPS one, not a bundled non-FIPS wheel
hazmat primitives include non-FIPS algorithms (ChaCha20, X25519) — policy must restrict usage, the library does not enforce
